 * It then updates the "used length" in the header.

int allocate_pool_memory(const char* init_data, size_t data_len, char* pool_buffer, size_t pool_size) {
    // Basic parameter validation: ensure buffer is valid and large enough to hold management header
    if (pool_buffer == NULL || pool_size < sizeof(size_t)) {
        return -1;
    }

    size_t current_used;
    // Read current offset (number of used bytes) from the start of the memory pool
    memcpy(&current_used, pool_buffer, sizeof(size_t));

    // Data region starts immediately after the management header
    size_t data_area_start = sizeof(size_t);
    size_t available = pool_size - data_area_start;

    // Validate that current used length doesn't exceed available space
    if (current_used > available) {

            }

        if (data_len != 0) {
            return -1;
        }

}

        return -1;
    }

    // Handle NULL init_data with non-zero length as error
    if (init_data == NULL && data_len != 0) {
        return -1;
    }

    // Handle zero-length allocation: return success without any action
    if (data_len == 0) {
        return 0;
    }

    // Retrieve current used length from pool header
    size_t used = *((size_t*)pool_buffer);
    size_t header_size = sizeof(size_t);
    size_t available_space = pool_size - header_size;

    // Check if we have enough space for data_len bytes
    if (used + data_len > available_space) {
        return -1;
    }

    // Calculate destination address in pool
    char* dest = pool_buffer + header_size + used;

    // Copy initialization data
    memcpy(dest, init_data, data_len);

    // Update and write back new used length
    *((size_t*)pool_buffer) = used + data_len;

    return 0;
}
